X.T: RPG fans irate as D&D tries to shut its “open” game license
X.ST: Leaked document seeks to revoke two decades of royalty-free rules sharing.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Wizards of the Coast wants to revoke their Open Gaming License.

X.T: Amid widespread backlash, D&D maker scales back “open” license changes
X.ST: License will remain royalty-free, earlier OGL content will “remain unaffected.”

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The uproar is such that WotC cancels their initial update.

X.T: Game makers stage mass exodus from Dungeons & Dragons’ “open” license
X.ST: WotC is “an existential threat to our game,” one publisher says.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: An overview of the reactions of the role play companies.

X.T: D&D maker promises to get player feedback for coming “open” license update
X.ST: WotC producer on leaked OGL v1.1: “We are sorry. We got it wrong.”

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Another answer of WotC.

X.T: D&D maker still wants to revoke earlier versions of “open” gaming license
X.ST: Core rules would go under Creative Commons, “hateful” content would be barred.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The story continues… yet another answer from WotC.

X.T: D&D maker retreats from attempts to update longstanding “open” license
X.ST: Negative response to latest draft was “in such high volume” as to force WotC’s hand.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: WotC has given up changing the licence.
